title: Suez Canal Bank (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Suez Canal Bank in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Suez Canal Bank?
faq.answer: Suez Canal Bank is a prominent Egyptian joint-stock commercial bank founded in 1978. Headquartered in Ismailia, Egypt, the bank has grown to become a significant player in the Egyptian banking sector, offering a wide range of financial services to individuals, businesses, and institutions.

faq.question: When was Suez Canal Bank founded?
faq.answer: Suez Canal Bank was established in 1978 under Investment Law No. 43 of 1974. The bank initially started with an authorized capital of EGP 10 million and a paid-up capital of EGP 2.5 million. Located in Ismailia, a strategic city near the Suez Canal, the bank was created to support Egypt's economic development during a period of economic reforms.

faq.question: Where is Suez Canal Bank headquartered?
faq.answer: The bank is headquartered in Ismailia, Egypt, with its main operations centered in this historically significant city. Suez Canal Bank has expanded its presence to include 50 branches across Egypt and a representative office in Tripoli, Libya.

faq.question: Where does Suez Canal Bank operate?
faq.answer: Suez Canal Bank primarily operates throughout Egypt, with branches in major cities including Cairo, Giza, Ismailia, Shubra, Hurghada, and other key economic centers. The bank also maintains a representative office in Tripoli, Libya, extending its international presence.

faq.question: What services does Suez Canal Bank offer?
faq.answer: The bank provides a comprehensive range of financial services, including: • Retail banking services • Corporate banking solutions • Islamic banking products • Personal and business loans • Savings and checking accounts • Investment banking • Trade finance • Credit cards • Foreign exchange services • Digital banking solutions (mobile wallet, online banking) • Financial advisory services

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Suez Canal Bank?
faq.answer: Suez Canal Bank is a commercial universal bank in Egypt, offering a diverse portfolio of financial services to both individual and corporate clients. It is a joint-stock company listed on the Cairo and Alexandria Stock Exchange, with a mix of institutional and public shareholders.

faq.question: What makes Suez Canal Bank unique?
faq.answer: Several factors distinguish Suez Canal Bank in the Egyptian banking landscape: • One of the first banks approved by the Central Bank of Egypt as a primary dealer in treasury bills and government bonds • Strong focus on digital transformation, including launching mobile wallet services and smart branches • Commitment to financial inclusion, offering services to underserved populations • Significant growth, achieving over EGP 1.36 billion in net profits by September 2023 • Recognized internationally, joining the Fortune 500 Arab list in 2023 • Extensive branch network of 50 branches across Egypt

faq.question: Shareholders
faq.answer: The bank's ownership structure includes: • Arab International Bank (41.48%) • Libyan Foreign Bank (27.21%) • Suez Canal Authority Employee Pension Fund (10.11%) • Arab Financial Investments Company (2.50%) • Public shareholders on the Egyptian Exchange (18.20%)

faq.question: Recent Achievements
faq.answer: Suez Canal Bank has demonstrated significant growth and innovation in recent years: • Achieved a 72% increase in net profits by the end of 2022 • Launched digital transformation initiatives, including robot-assisted branches • Expanded partnerships, including agreements with Allianz Egypt and NEWGIZA Sports Club • Continued to strengthen its position in the Egyptian and Arab banking markets
